title: Machine First Planning Chain First Slice 2026 04 23
goal: Prove a canonical machine-first execplan record can own promotion, summary, archive, and shipped planning payload surfaces.
non_goals: Do not close the full machine-first planning lane in one slice.
Do not introduce a hidden second planning store outside checked-in execplan surfaces.

intent.outcome: Land the first bounded machine-first execplan slice without widening into a full planning-chain rewrite.
intent.constraints: Keep canonical state in checked-in execplan sidecars and keep Markdown reviewable as the derived human view.
intent.latitude: Adjust installer, tests, and package docs where needed to make the canonical sidecar path durable.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the slice would need a second hidden store or break git-friendly reviewability.

execution.next_step: Keep the lane active and continue converting remaining Markdown-authored planning truth into canonical records.
execution.proof: Focused planning package tests, package install refresh, planning surface checks, and boundary validation pass.

invariants: Canonical machine-first state stays checked in beside the human view rather than in a separate hidden store.
Active and archived execplans remain git-reviewable.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Replace prose-first authored execplans with a genuinely machine-first canonical planning state and derived human-readable views.

required_continuation.activation trigger: Continue when any remaining planning truth still depends on Markdown-authored or Markdown-only state.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Canonical `.plan.json` sidecars now drive promote, summary, archive, and installed planning surfaces.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Wider machine-first adoption across remaining planning artifacts and any archive/report surfaces not yet backed by canonical records.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The renderer needed an explicit derived-path rule so the human view stays at `*.md` instead of drifting to `*.plan.md`.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Focused planning tests and repo-level planning checks passed with the canonical sidecar path enabled.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Real repo dogfooding across more than one slice and any additional extraction of Markdown-authored planning truth.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Promote another bounded planning surface that still authors truth in Markdown and move it behind canonical machine-first data.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Implement the first machine-first planning-chain slice and keep the larger lane honest.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not add a hidden planning store and do not break the current human-readable planning review path.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Package docs/tests/install refresh and the exact bounded canonical execplan shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The slice would need a broader planning storage rewrite or would invalidate current archive/report routing.

active_milestone.scope: Canonical execplan sidecars for active and archived planning state, with derived Markdown views.

immediate_next_action: Use the new canonical execplan sidecar path on the next real planning slice and keep extracting remaining Markdown-authored planning truth.
invariants: Canonical planning state stays checked in.
Markdown remains the derived human-readable view when a canonical sidecar exists.
completion_criteria: Canonical execplan sidecars are part of the shipped planning contract.
Promotion and archive flows create and preserve the canonical record alongside the human-readable view.
Planning summary prefers the canonical sidecar when present.

execution_run.what happened: Added canonical execplan sidecars, rendered human views from them, updated planning docs, fixed derived-path rendering, and extended test coverage.
execution_run.scope touched: planning package source, tests, and payload docs
execution_run.changed surfaces: packages/planning source, tests, payload docs, and installed planning templates
execution_run.validations run: Focused planning tests, packaging test, planning-surface check, source/payload/install boundary check, and both root upgrades.
execution_run.result for continuation: The first slice is complete; keep the lane active for broader machine-first adoption.
execution_run.next step: Continue with the next bounded planning surface still authored in Markdown.

finished_run_review.config compliance: No special config changes were required for this bounded slice.

proof_report.validation proof: Focused planning package tests plus repo-level planning checks passed after the canonical sidecar path shipped.
proof_report.proof achieved now: The planning package can now treat `.plan.json` as canonical execplan state with derived `.md` views.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Promotion, summary, archive, packaging, and root-install surfaces all accepted the canonical sidecar path.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement and commit `machine-first-planning-chain` as the next lane.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: A real shipped first slice landed, but the larger machine-first lane remains open by design.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The first canonical execplan slice is done, but the lane still has remaining machine-first planning work.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Roadmap priority now keeps `machine-first-planning-chain` as the front candidate lane.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Any remaining planning truth still authored primarily in Markdown.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Shipped the first canonical execplan sidecar path and refreshed the installed planning payload to match.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused planning tests, package packaging test, planning-surface check, source/payload/install boundary check, and both root upgrades passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Keep using canonical execplan sidecars as the proving path before widening the machine-first chain further.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (memory/docs/config): Planning docs and templates now encode the canonical-sidecar contract.
execution_summary.resume from: Promote the next bounded planning surface still relying on Markdown-authored state.

drift_log: 2026-04-23: Added canonical execplan sidecars, derived Markdown rendering, shipped template/docs updates, and first-slice planning residue.
